name: BeadCloseable
description:
Library component. Variant of Bead with HOOK and CLOSE in-ports. Uses enum status states (Open / Hooked / Closed) instead of typed-int because Kinner observe-port semantics don't currently expose typed-int variables to a remote observer -- the SlingWithCheck component needs to observe a sibling Bead's state to gate its hooking, which requires enum states.

The original Bead component (lib/bead.kinner.json) uses typed-int status for the bug spec where status is consulted only by the actor itself (in its own triples) and by the project-level invariant. BeadCloseable uses enum status because the SlingWithCheck observe-gate needs cross-actor visibility.

Used by sling-3768-fix.kinner.json (the proposed-fix model). The CLOSE port is idempotent: a Close on an already-Open or already-Closed bead is consumed but doesn't transition state. Only a Hooked bead transitions to Closed on Close.

HOOK.description: receives a hook event when a sling targets this bead. Tagged 'Sling'. Only fires when this bead is in state Open.

CLOSE.description: receives a close event from a sibling sling. Tagged 'Close'. Transitions Hooked -> Closed; consumed as no-op on Open or Closed.

actors.name: BeadCloseable
actors.owner: Single bead's lifecycle as enum states (Open / Hooked / Closed) plus a typed-int formula identifier for cross-formula invariant disambiguation.

triples.event: sling targets this bead: open -> hooked
sibling sling closes this hooked bead: hooked -> closed
close on open bead consumed but no-op
close on already-closed bead consumed but no-op
